Class Util

java.lang.Object
primitives.Util
public final class Util extends Object
Collection of low-level utility methods used throughout the primitives package.

The class provides operations related to floating-point accuracy control, sign comparison, and simple numeric utilities.

The implementation is optimized for performance and therefore avoids expensive floating-point operations where possible, relying instead on direct manipulation of the IEEE-754 double representation.

This class cannot be instantiated.
Author:
Dan Zilberstein

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    private static final int
    ACCURACY
    Accuracy threshold expressed in exponent units.
    private static final int
    EXPONENT_BIAS
    Bias used in the IEEE-754 double exponent representation (1023).
    private static final long
    EXPONENT_MASK
    Bit mask used to isolate the exponent field.
    private static final int
    EXPONENT_SHIFT
    Number of mantissa bits in a double (used to extract the exponent).

  • Field Details

    • ACCURACY

      private static final int ACCURACY
      Accuracy threshold expressed in exponent units. Values whose exponent is smaller than this threshold are considered zero. Roughly corresponds to about 1e-12.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • EXPONENT_BIAS

      private static final int EXPONENT_BIAS
      Bias used in the IEEE-754 double exponent representation (1023).
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • EXPONENT_SHIFT

      private static final int EXPONENT_SHIFT
      Number of mantissa bits in a double (used to extract the exponent).
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • EXPONENT_MASK

      private static final long EXPONENT_MASK
      Bit mask used to isolate the exponent field.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

  • Constructor Details

    • Util

      private Util()
      Don't let anyone instantiate this class.

  • Method Details

    • getExp

      private static int getExp(double num)
      Extracts the unbiased exponent of a double.

      IEEE-754 double format: 

      sign | exponent (11 bits) | mantissa (52 bits)
      The actual number represented is: 
      value = mantissa * 2 ^ exponent
      where the stored exponent is biased by 1023.

      Parameters:
      num - the number
      Returns:
      the unbiased exponent

    • isZero

      public static boolean isZero(double number)
      Checks whether a number is effectively zero, within the configured accuracy.
      Parameters:
      number - the number to check
      Returns:
      true if the number is considered zero

    • alignZero

      public static double alignZero(double number)
      Returns zero if the given value is numerically close to zero.

      This helps eliminate floating-point noise that may accumulate in geometric calculations.

      Parameters:
      number - the number to align
      Returns:
      0.0 if the value is considered zero, otherwise the original value

    • compareSign

      public static boolean compareSign(double n1, double n2)
      Checks whether two numbers have the same sign.

      Zero is considered neither positive nor negative.

      Parameters:
      n1 - first number
      n2 - second number
      Returns:
      true if both numbers are positive or both are negative

    • random

      public static double random(double min, double max)
      Returns a random double in the range [min, max).
      Parameters:
      min - lower bound (inclusive)
      max - upper bound (exclusive)
      Returns:
      random value in the specified range
